question: What does Ch.01 state about the NuScale containment design, and what specific CNV dimensions or pressure ratings does Ch.05 provide?

nuscale_ch01_0167.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.1 Primary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0167.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3 requirements for primary containment, as its passive containment vessel does not include active fission product clean-up or pressure suppression systems; instead, fission product control is achieved through inherent design features. Criteria related to secondary containment and other fission product control systems are not applicable, since NuScale lacks these systems and relies solely on its primary containment vessel and associated passive barriers.

nuscale_ch01_0070.title: Legends and Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0070.summary: This section provides legends and symbols used in NuScale's Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including detailed explanations for electrical symbols found in single-line diagrams. It describes the notation and labeling conventions for various components such as transformers, circuit breakers, batteries, switches, and monitors, ensuring clarity and consistency in interpreting technical diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and design of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and other chapters for further information on radioactive waste management and building layouts.

nuscale_ch01_0168.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.2 Secondary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0168.summary: The NuScale design does not include secondary containment or fission product clean-up systems, as its passive safety features and submerged containment vessel inherently control fission products. Therefore, regulatory criteria related to secondary containment and certain fission product control systems are not applicable to the NuScale Power Module, and only specific containment parameters such as leakage rates are relevant for review.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design with passive safety systems that do not rely on electrical power. The NPM is partially immersed in a reactor pool, has dedicated emergency cooling and decay heat removal systems, and allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron.

nuscale_ch01_0109.title: Figure 1.7-1a: Electrical Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0109.summary: This section provides a legend for electrical symbols used in NuScale's Final Safety Analysis Report single-line diagrams. It explains the meaning of various symbols and annotations for electrical components such as transformers, circuit breakers, batteries, switches, and relays, detailing how to interpret ratings, quantities, and connection types. This ensures consistent understanding and accurate reading of the electrical diagrams throughout the report.

nuscale_ch01_0706.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.B Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0706.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, closure of the main steam isolation valve, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), specifically regarding fuel cladding integrity. It notes that the NuScale design does not include a steam pressure regulator and that there are no exceptions or non-conformances identified for incidents of moderate frequency, instrument setpoints, limiting single failures, or performance of nonsafety-related systems.

nuscale_ch01_0048.title: Security Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0048.summary: The Security Buildings (SCBs) at a NuScale Power Plant are nonsafety-related structures that include the primary access control building. These buildings support plant security functions but are not classified as safety-related structures within the facility.

nuscale_ch01_0709.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.E Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0709.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for various postulated events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), although the design does not include a steam pressure regulator. For each scenario, including the most limiting plant system single failure, the design is shown to conform with requirements, and no exceptions or additional comments are noted.

nuscale_ch01_0023.title: Containment Systems
nuscale_ch01_0023.summary: The NuScale containment vessel (CNV) is kept under vacuum during normal operations, which reduces moisture and corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits oxygen for safety. In the event of an emergency, passive cooling rapidly reduces pressure and temperature, and the design’s reliance on passive safety systems and small fuel inventory results in improved safety and a lower core damage frequency compared to traditional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, but do not contain any safety-related systems or support engineered safety functions (ESFs). Their primary purpose is to provide support and housing for the generators, associated equipment, and maintenance areas.

nuscale_ch01_0007.title: Schedule
nuscale_ch01_0007.summary: The section describes the NuScale containment vessel as a cylindrical, ASME-certified steel structure designed to withstand high-pressure events, with the reactor and containment integrated into a single module. The NuScale reactor is a passive, small modular pressurized water reactor with natural circulation, eliminating the need for coolant pumps, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding a net output of approximately 570 MWe. The schedule requirement states that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide their own project schedules.

nuscale_ch01_0747.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0747.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2, regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system for PWRs. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant regulatory criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, and analysis methods, while noting that certain guidance is not applicable due to the absence of reactor coolant pumps and that operator action is not required for boron dilution events.

nuscale_ch01_0748.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0748.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system, specifically addressing fuel cladding integrity. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant SRP 15.4.6 requirements, noting that Critical Heat Flux (CHF) is the appropriate term for the NuScale phenomenon, and that operator action is not required to mitigate such events.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel (CNV). The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release, supports various reactor components, and is constructed from materials selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with specific classifications for different piping systems, and references the applicable ASME BPVC 2013 Edition as the code of record.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor materials, water chemistry, and safety requirements.
